title: Chapter 12 - Semantic HTML
description: Check understanding of semantic elements like header, nav, main, section, article, footer.

questions.prompt: Which element is intended for the top of a page or section?

explanations.overview: <header> is used for introductory content or navigational aids at the top.

byOptionId.header: Correct — <header> is intended for the top area of a page or section.
byOptionId.footer: Incorrect — <footer> belongs at the end of a page or section.
byOptionId.nav: Incorrect — <nav> is for navigation links, often inside a header, but not the header itself.
byOptionId.div: Incorrect — <div> is generic; semantic elements like <header> are preferred when appropriate.

questions.prompt: Which element should appear only once and contain the central content?

explanations.overview: <main> contains the main content of the document and should be unique per page.

byOptionId.main: Correct — <main> designates the central content unique to the page.
byOptionId.article: Incorrect — <article> represents a self-contained composition, not necessarily the whole main content.
byOptionId.section: Incorrect — <section> is a thematic grouping and can appear multiple times.
byOptionId.nav: Incorrect — <nav> is for navigation links, not the main content.
